title: GUARANA
description: Guarana (Paullinia cupana) is an Amazonian plant rich in methylxanthines, particularly caffeine, and polyphenols like catechins and epicatechins. It functions as a central nervous system stimulant and antioxidant, widely used to enhance cognitive performance, reduce fatigue, and support metabolic health.

research.title: Effects of the consumption of guarana on human health: A narrative review
research.summary: A comprehensive review highlighting guarana's stimulating, anti-inflammatory, and anti-obesity effects driven by its high content of methylxanthines and flavonoids.

research.title: Cognitive effects of guarana supplementation with maximal intensity cycling
research.summary: Research demonstrating that guarana significantly improves reaction times and cognitive performance both before and after high-intensity physical exercise.

research.title: Guarana provides additional stimulation over caffeine alone in the planarian model
research.summary: Laboratory evidence suggesting that the complex phytochemistry of guarana seeds produces a stronger stimulant effect than anhydrous caffeine alone.

research.title: The use of guarana (Paullinia cupana) as a dietary supplement for fatigue in cancer patients
research.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis evaluating guarana's effectiveness in managing physiological fatigue in clinical populations.

research.title: Effects of Guarana and Green Tea Consumption on Students' Intellectual Performances
research.summary: A 2025 study found that daily guarana consumption improved reaction speed, memory, and attention scores in university students.

sentiment.summary: Users overwhelmingly prefer guarana over coffee for its 'smoother' energy delivery and lack of a harsh 'crash,' noting it provides sustained alertness for hours.

effects.label: Physical Synergies:
effects.description: Highly effective when taken before exercise to maintain cognitive focus under physical strain.

effects.label: Comparison to Caffeine:
effects.description: Both research and users suggest guarana out-performs pure caffeine due to its secondary bioactive compounds.

effects.label: Fatigue Reduction:
effects.description: Proven effective in reducing both mental fatigue from studying and physical fatigue in clinical settings.

dosage.label: Standard Powder Dose:
dosage.description: Common supplemental doses range from 500 mg to 2000 mg of seed powder daily.

dosage.label: Extract Ratios:
dosage.description: High-potency extracts are often standardized to 10-22% caffeine, requiring much lower gram dosages.

dosage.label: Incremental Dosing:
dosage.description: Users recommend starting with small doses (200-500 mg) to assess individual sensitivity to its potent stimulant effects.

sideEffects.label: Insomnia:
sideEffects.description: Due to its long half-life, evening consumption can significantly disrupt sleep patterns.

sideEffects.label: Tachycardia:
sideEffects.description: Excess intake may lead to a rapid heart rate or palpitations in sensitive individuals.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Issues:
sideEffects.description: Some users report mild stomach upset if taken on an empty stomach without adequate hydration.

availability.label: GRAS Status:
availability.description: Generally Recognized As Safe (GRAS) by the FDA when used as a flavoring or dietary ingredient.

availability.label: Market Forms:
availability.description: Widely available as a bulk powder, encapsulated extract, or a key ingredient in 'natural' energy drinks.
